architecture_summary: Twins revisits spatial attention in vision transformers with two backbones: Twins-PCPVT, which replaces absolute positional encodings in PVT with conditional positional encodings, and Twins-SVT, which interleaves locally-grouped self-attention (LSA) and global sub-sampled attention (GSA) as a spatially separable self-attention design.

novel_contributions: We revisit the design of spatial attention in vision transformers and show that a carefully devised yet simple spatial attention mechanism performs favorably against state-of-the-art schemes.
We propose two vision transformer architectures, namely, Twins-PCPVT and Twins-SVT.
Twins-PCPVT replaces absolute positional encodings in PVT with conditional positional encodings and can match or outperform Swin.
Twins-SVT introduces spatially separable self-attention composed of LSA and GSA, making the architecture efficient and easy to implement.
The proposed architectures achieve excellent performance on image-level classification, dense detection, and segmentation.

key_claims.claim: The paper proposes two vision transformer backbones, Twins-PCPVT and Twins-SVT, by revisiting spatial attention design.
Twins-PCPVT uses conditional positional encodings in PVT and can perform on par with or better than state-of-the-art vision transformers such as Swin.
Twins-SVT interleaves locally-grouped self-attention and global sub-sampled attention as a spatially separable self-attention mechanism.
On ImageNet-1K, Twins-PCPVT-S obtains 81.2% top-1 and Twins-SVT-L obtains 83.7% top-1.
On ADE20K, Twins-SVT-L achieves 50.2% mIoU under comparable FLOPs and is described as a new state of the art result.
On COCO val2017, Twins-SVT-L achieves 45.7 AP with RetinaNet 1× and 45.9 AP with Mask R-CNN 1× in the large-model results.
All classification models are trained for 300 epochs with batch size 1024 using AdamW, cosine decay, warmup, stochastic depth, and gradient clipping.
